domain.name: recruiting_ops
domain.description: Recruiting operations — candidate screening, interview scheduling & structured notes (use case #9). A RecruitAgent grounds on a Role's requirements + a submitted Application/resume, PROPOSES a screening score, schedules interviews, and drafts structured interview notes. It never finalizes an advance/reject — it writes a proposed Decision. Scheduling and note drafting auto-apply; the advance/reject decision ALWAYS enters an OutSystems approval workflow (BPT) for a human recruiter/hiring_manager to sign off before the write lands — a candidate is never auto-rejected. Every action is audited. The bounded action space (screen-and-score | schedule-interview | draft-structured-notes) + the human-always-at-the-decision-step is the fairness contract that makes it ship, not just reason.

capabilities.name: SubmitApplication
capabilities.note: Intake — records a candidate's application against a role and kicks off the screening/scheduling flow.

capabilities.name: ScreenApplication
capabilities.note: THE AGENT. Grounds on the Role's Requirements/Rubric + the Application/Candidate resume (native retrieval tool), PROPOSES a screening score (ScreeningResult), schedules interviews (Interview), and drafts structured interview notes (InterviewNote) — the bounded action space (screen-and-score | schedule-interview | draft-structured-notes). It also writes a PROPOSED Decision (advance|reject) with a RiskTier, but never finalizes it — a candidate is never auto-rejected; the human signs the advance/reject.

capabilities.name: RecordDecision
capabilities.note: The bounded WRITE to the system of record: finalize the proposed Decision (advance/reject), advance the Application stage, mark the Decision recorded, audited. Called by the workflow ONLY after a human approves — never auto for a reject.

capabilities.name: ScheduleInterview
capabilities.note: The other bounded write: confirm/persist a proposed interview slot on the calendar, audited. Auto-applies (scheduling is not the gated decision) but can be adjusted by a recruiter.

capabilities.name: ScreeningWorkflow
capabilities.note: HITL via OutSystems BPT: on ApplicationSubmitted the workflow runs the RecruitAgent to screen-and-score, propose a Decision, and draft notes; the Schedule step auto-calls ScheduleInterview (scheduling & note drafting are auto). The advance/reject Decision ALWAYS holds at a Human Approval activity until a recruiter/hiring_manager approves — a candidate is never auto-rejected — THEN calls RecordDecision. The approval + audit is native workflow, not custom code — the fairness gate.

capabilities.name: ApprovalInbox
capabilities.note: Where a recruiter/hiring_manager approves/rejects a held advance/reject proposal — the write-step sign-off. The agent proposes; a human always signs the advance/reject.

capabilities.name: ManageRoles
capabilities.note: Admin maintains the roles + screening rubrics the agent grounds on (Requirements, Rubric, Status).

integrations.name: AtsSystem
integrations.purpose: optional external applicant-tracking system (Greenhouse/Lever-style REST) the SubmitApplication/RecordDecision services can sync to and the agent can retrieve candidate/resume context from

integrations.name: Calendar
integrations.purpose: calendar/scheduling provider (Google Calendar/Outlook-style REST) the ScheduleInterview service books interview slots on
